mentions.note: Housing sector showing green shoots; pricing and mortgage rates down; potential 401k home-purchase rule could send homebuilder stocks much higher.
Greenland peace dividend lifts homebuilder; crushed yesterday on tariff/war fears, rallies today on relief.
After dry spell, getting big biotech orders; better managed quarters recently; stock down today could be opportunity. First truly strong quarter in years possible.
Sole bright spot in home-builder sector on Day 2; housing needs rate cuts to sustain rally.
Down 3.3%; lower-end homebuilder also down on group downgrade; housing sector weakness broad.
Low-priced home builder; likely beneficiary of rate cuts if Kevin Warsh Fed delivers; housing should explode post-war.

Largest US homebuilder; Q1 mixed (missed deliveries, home revenue down 1.9% YoY) but forward metrics crushed (net sales +11% units, backlog +19% units). Management upbeat on demand despite minimal rate relief. Stock rallied 6% post-earnings on forward guidance beat; dependent on further rate cuts for sustainability.
